earlyVoting.notes: Early voting hours vary by county. At least 9 days of early voting required by law. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
Early voting hours vary by county. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
Early voting is required by law; at least 9 days. Hours vary by county. TODO verify exact 2026 dates.

votingRules.phonesAtPollsDetail: New York voters may use phones to refer to voting information. Taking photos inside the voting booth is not permitted.
additionalRules: Closed primary: only voters enrolled in a party may vote in that party's primary. Unaffiliated voters may not vote in partisan primaries.
Party enrollment change deadline is 25 days before the general election to take effect for the following year's primary. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.

eligibility: Will be absent from county on Election Day and during the entire early voting period
Temporary or permanent illness or physical disability
Primary caretaker of one or more ill or disabled persons
Patient or inmate in a Veterans Administration hospital
Detained in jail awaiting grand jury action or trial, or confined under a sentence for a conviction that is not a felony
voteByMail.applicationDeadline: 15 days before the election (received by board of elections)
voteByMail.returnDeadline: Received by the board of elections no later than the close of polls on Election Day, or postmarked by Election Day and received within 7 days after
voteByMail.returnDeadlinePlain: Must arrive by poll closing on Election Day, or be postmarked by Election Day and received within 7 days after.
